intro: Migrating from C to C# moves a developer from low-level procedural hardware tracking into Microsoft's modern, safe, and highly optimized .NET application runtime ecosystem. This transition replaces dangerous pointer operations and manual memory cleanup routines with object-oriented encapsulation, comprehensive framework libraries, and a reliable garbage collector. Developers adopt C# to build secure cloud APIs, backend microservices, or complex enterprise business logic tools rapidly.

diffs.from: Statically typed, weakly checked procedural primitives with no safety boundaries protecting memory boundaries.
diffs.to: Statically typed, nominal system featuring reified generics, unified object hierarchies, and true value-type structures.

diffs.from: Procedural code layouts organized inside separate file headers, manual structures, and function sequences.
diffs.to: Modern, expression-dense syntax utilizing properties, rich pattern matching, classes, and native string interpolation patterns.

diffs.from: Manually handled via explicit stack placement or precise dynamic heap allocation and deallocation code.
diffs.to: Fully managed runtime allocation environment relying on automatic garbage collection, optimized with low-overhead stack structs.

diffs.from: No built-in language abstractions, relying entirely on platform-specific thread configurations (like pthreads).
diffs.to: Language-level async/await primitives backed by the Task Parallel Library (TPL) compiler state-machines.

diffs.from: Minimalist library managing platform math, raw string calculations, files, and core memory streams.
diffs.to: The unified .NET Base Class Library (BCL), known for unified LINQ processing pipelines and highly optimized modern Web I/O APIs.

examples.title: Safe Data Properties
examples.description: Refactoring manual C structs into clean, type-safe C# classes utilizing automatic public property accessors.

examples.title: Safe Memory Buffer Reading
examples.description: Converting unsafe C pointer array scans into safe, modern C# managed collection parsing pipelines.

tips.title: Stop Using Raw Pointers
tips.body: Pointers are unnecessary in C#. Pass object references directly and let the garbage collector manage memory cleanup paths safely.

tips.title: Leverage Public Properties
tips.body: Avoid building explicit getter and setter methods. Use C#'s concise public property fields { get; set; } to manage data exposure cleanly.

tips.title: Handle Errors via Exceptions
tips.body: Stop passing back integer success codes. Use structured try-catch blocks and throw explicit exceptions when operations fail.

tips.title: Use LINQ for Processing
tips.body: Replace custom data searching and filtering loops with clean, declaration-based LINQ queries to parse collections concisely.
